title: Decoder producer/ranker r64 k1 ready-valid equivalence
hypothesis: Before measuring a producer-integrated ranker macro, the first r64/k1 target should prove the ready-valid stream contract against generated RTL blocks. A deterministic RTL harness tying logit_rank_r64_l16_k1 to candidate_stream_merge_fifo_k1_l16_t16_d16 should match the full-vocabulary reference for accepted beat count, lower-token tie order, FIFO observables, and final last-beat completion.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the first r64/k1 producer-to-ranker stream target preserve the perf-sim/full-vocabulary ranking semantics under the ready-valid contract?
include: accepted beat count, FIFO occupancy, stall count, and final completion observables
nm16 producer config as measured MAC-context metadata

expected_benefit: adds the missing equivalence gate before PPA is charged to the producer-integrated ranker path
keeps the first integrated target at r64/k1 and tied to the nm16 producer context
separates stream-contract correctness from later physical macro measurement
risks: the harness feeds deterministic logit tiles directly rather than connecting the full producer GEMM
the wrapper is a functional integration harness, not the final floorplanned macro
randomized backpressure and deeper buffering remain follow-up work

required_evaluations.objective: Run the first r64/k1 producer-to-ranker ready-valid RTL equivalence harness and record stream-contract observables against a full-vocabulary reference.

expected_result.reason: If the stream harness passes, the next measured job can be a bounded r64/k1 producer-integrated ranker macro. If it fails, fix stream contract or RTL integration before physical measurement.
